concept.title: AI-Readiness Maturity Model for Content
concept.shortdesc: A five-level maturity model for evaluating how ready your content is for AI-driven delivery — from Level 1 (unstructured files) to Level 5 (fully intelligent, self-optimizing content).

section.title: The five levels of content AI-readiness

row.entry: Flat files (Word, wiki pages, flat XML/HTML). No metadata. No typing. Visual formatting embedded in content.
Keyword search only. No filtering, no intent routing, no personalization.
Content organized into typed units (concepts, tasks, references). Semantic markup replaces visual formatting. Basic metadata present.
Vector search with section-aware chunking. Type-based answer formatting. Basic filtering by category.
Full metadata on every topic: audience, difficulty, domain, intent, priority. Controlled vocabulary via taxonomy. Section IDs enabling deep linking.
Metadata-filtered retrieval. Intent-based answer routing. Audience-matched results. Priority-weighted ranking.
Content linked into learning paths, prerequisite chains, and relationship tables. Glossary with synonym resolution. Schema.org structured data output.
Follow-up suggestions. "Read this first" guidance. Term resolution. Web-standard structured data for search engines.
Analytics-instrumented output. Feedback loops. Content automatically improves based on usage data, retrieval performance, and user ratings.
Self-optimizing content delivery. Data-driven gap analysis. Automated quality scoring. Continuous improvement pipeline.

section.title: How to score your content's AI-readiness
section.p: Evaluate your content across ten dimensions, scoring each from 1 (absent) to 10 (excellent):

ol.li: Structural intelligence: Are topics properly typed (concept, task, reference)? Do sections have IDs and titles? Is there a clear information hierarchy?
Metadata completeness: Does every topic have audience, difficulty, domain, intent, priority, dates, and keywords?

AI/RAG pipeline readiness: Is there an ingestion pipeline? Chunking strategy? Vector database? Retrieval configuration? (RAG — Retrieval Augmented Generation — is a pattern where an AI retrieves relevant content chunks from a database before generating an answer, rather than relying solely on its training data.)

Taxonomy and controlled vocabulary: Is there a formal subject scheme (a DITA file that defines the allowed metadata values — like a picklist for categories) or other classification system? Are values consistent across topics?
Glossary and term resolution: Is there a machine-readable glossary with synonyms, abbreviations, and usage guidance?

Schema.org / structured data: Do published outputs include JSON-LD (a lightweight format for embedding machine-readable metadata in HTML pages) or other structured data markup that search engines can consume?
Retrieval optimization: Are shortdescs written as chunk summaries? Section titles as questions? Content front-loaded?
Reuse architecture: Is content single-sourced via conref/keyref? Are there shared libraries?
Consistency across corpus: Is the metadata standard applied uniformly? Are there gaps or two-tier quality?
Scalability and governance: Can the content model scale to 10x the current corpus? Are there authoring guidelines and validation?

section.p: A score below 5/10 on any dimension indicates a critical gap that will degrade AI-driven delivery. The target for production chatbot deployment is 8+/10 on all dimensions.

section.title: Case study: The Extense training guide assessment
section.p: When we assessed the Extense DITA Training Guide against this model, the initial scoring was:

ul.li: Overall: 7.2/10 — strong foundation, but a critical gap in metadata consistency
Structural intelligence: 9/10 — proper information typing throughout
Metadata completeness: 6/10 — 15 top-level topics had full metadata, but 34 sub-topics had bare-minimum metadata (author + indexterms only)
Consistency: 5/10 — the two-tier metadata gap meant 69% of topics were invisible to metadata-filtered retrieval

section.p: After enriching all 49 topics with full AI-ready prolog metadata:

ul.li: Metadata completeness: 9/10 — 100% of topics carry full AI-ready metadata
Consistency: 9/10 — uniform standard across all topic tiers

section.p: This guide documents every step of that transformation.
